title: Pasania daphnoidea
intro: Pasania daphnoidea, also known as Padauk or Burmese Padauk, is a deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The health benefits of Pasania daphnoidea include its potential to treat fever, inflammation, and various infections, as well as exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pasania daphnoidea is commonly used in traditional medicine, and its bioactive compounds are extracted and formulated into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Pasania daphnoidea is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. It is native to Asia and belongs to the genus Pasania. The classification of Pasania daphnoidea is further divided into its kingdom, which is Plantae, and its rank, which is species.
